definition: Average EBITDA addbacks at ~25-30% of reported EBITDA often represent projected labor rationalization synergies that are unrealizable in tight labor markets where replacement workers are unavailable. Covenant-lite share (~87% of issuance) means lenders cannot intervene when synergies fail to materialize.
evidence: S&P LCD data: average EBITDA addbacks at ~25-30% of reported EBITDA
Significant portion represents projected cost synergies — often labor rationalization
Covenant-lite share ~87% of issuance prevents lender intervention
Validated in debate as sharp and actionable
open_questions: What share of addbacks specifically relate to labor synergies vs other cost categories?
Which sectors have highest exposure to unrealizable labor synergies?
How does this interact with the maturity wall timing?
